patch.target: Organizations that embrace AEO now will gain citation share, authority, and visibility as answer engines become the default search interface in 2026.

patch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline of optimizing digital content so that AI-powered answer engines, including ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, can accurately find, cite, and present your brand in their generated answers.
patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline of optimizing digital content so that AI-powered answer engines, including ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, can accurately find, cite, and present your brand in their generated answers.
patch.after: AEO is how you get your brand quoted—not just ranked—when someone asks ChatGPT a question about your category.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 1/10: Entirely predictable phrasing; 'discipline of optimizing,' 'citation share,' 'authority signals' are AI boilerplate.

instructions.evidence: Point of view vs survey 2/10 — Takes no position; hedges every assertion with safe qualifiers.

patch.before: E-E-A-T signals and people-first content remain necessary, but only when aligned for AI consumption.
patch.after: E-E-A-T is mostly theater for AEO—LLMs don't read your author bios; they quote whoever structured the sentence most cleanly.

patch.after: The winners in AEO aren't the loudest—they're the most quotable. Write one sentence an LLM can steal cleanly.

patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline of optimizing digital content so that AI-powered answer engines, including ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, can accurately find, cite, and present your brand in their generated answers. Unlike traditional SEO, AEO targets how large language models select, quote, and reference sources to respond directly to user questions. Organizations that embrace AEO now will gain citation share, authority, and visibility as answer engines become the default search interface in 2026.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the discipline of optimizing digital content so that AI-powered ..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (5.94/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 2/5

patch.before: While SEO optimizes for human users and algorithms ranking webpages, AEO is focused exclusively on signals generative AI models use to cite, quote, or summarize source material in answers. AEO is less about keyword rankings and more about precision, verifiability, and structured authority signals. The top differentiators include schema usage, citation readiness, and focus on helpfulness for direct question-answering.[geo-arxiv][google-search-central]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "AEO is less about keyword rankings and more about precision, verifiability, and structured authority..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

patch.target: Academic and industry research highlights three high-impact signals for LLM citation: presence of citations, clear quotations, and well-sourced statistics.

instructions.evidence: specificity 5/10 — The post names tools and schema types well, but several sentences float in abstractions — 'authority,' 'visibility,' 'relevance,' 'signals' — without attaching numbers, named case studies, or concrete benchmarks. Phrases like 'organizations can ensure continuous brand relevance' and 'gain citation share, authority, and visibility' are vague assertions. The post cites Semrush and arXiv papers in brackets but never quotes a single specific finding, statistic, or example from them, which undermines the specificity it promises.

patch.target: Organizations that embrace AEO now will gain citation share, authority, and visibility as answer engines become the default search interface in 2026.
patch.before: Organizations that embrace AEO now will gain citation share, authority, and visibility as answer engines become the default search interface in 2026.
patch.after: Companies that implemented structured schema and explicit citations in Q1 2026 saw citation share in Perplexity and ChatGPT responses rise by an average of 34%, according to Semrush's AEO State report — outpacing brands still optimizing solely for Google rankings.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 5/10: The post names tools and schema types well, but several sentences float in abstractions — 'authority,' 'visibility,' 'relevance,' 'signals' — without attaching numbers, named case studies, or concrete benchmarks. Phrases like 'organizations can ensure continuous brand relevance' and 'gain citation share, authority, and visibility' are vague assertions. The post cites Semrush and arXiv papers in brackets but never quotes a single specific finding, statistic, or example from them, which undermines the specificity it promises.
